nativeRange.habitat: Seasonal marshes, rice paddies, and muddy stream margins of the Western Ghats and Deccan plateau of southwestern India. The species is primarily a marginal/terrestrial plant during the wet season that has been adapted to submerged aquarium culture.

decorPreferences: background or rear-midground placement in tight bushy groups
medium-to-high PAR lighting to maintain compact form
regular trimming to promote bushy growth and lateral branching

summary: An Indian stem plant in the mint family prized for dense, conifer-like vertical spires of narrow needle-shaped leaves; a striking background accent that demands strong light, CO2, and regular trimming to stay compact.
careNotes: Pogostemon erectus is an Indian stem plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) famous in the aquascaping hobby for its distinctive conifer-like vertical spires of narrow, needle-shaped bright-green leaves whorled tightly around a central stem. It is one of the most recognizable Pogostemon species in the hobby and forms a strong vertical architectural element when planted in dense groups as a background or rear-midground feature. A taxonomic note that matters in trade: the plant universally sold in the hobby as 'Pogostemon erectus' since the mid-2000s is now widely accepted (Tropica, Flowgrow) to actually be Pogostemon deccanensis (Panigrahi) Press, with the true Dalzell-described P. erectus being a marginal/terrestrial plant that does not adapt well to submerged aquarium culture. The 'P. erectus' name remains the universal trade label and is kept here as the primary species name, with P. deccanensis listed as a synonym. Native range is southwestern India (Western Ghats and Deccan plateau), where it grows in seasonal marshes, rice paddies, and muddy stream margins. Care is generally rated medium (Buce Plant) to medium-advanced (Tropica) to difficult (Flowgrow); the species is set here as 'advanced' to reflect that it demands strong light, CO2, and nutrient-rich substrate to stay compact and avoid lower-leaf melt. Height is typically 15-40 cm (Tropica) — substantially smaller than its sister P. stellatus — and the species stays denser under stronger light (Tropica explicitly notes 'intense light helps the plant to stay compact for a longer period'). Comfortable temperature range is approximately 22-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH 2-15, kH 1-8). CO2 is strongly recommended (Tropica cites a 6-14 mg/L injection range) and the plant rewards iron and full micronutrient dosing with deep green coloration and tight whorled growth. Frequent pruning is required to keep the plant in shape; without it, lower stems shed leaves and the bushy form deteriorates. Best presented in tight groups of multiple stems for visual impact rather than as solitary specimens. Unlike its red-pigmented sister P. stellatus, P. erectus stays a vibrant green under all conditions and is grown primarily for form and texture rather than color.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings. Trim the apical 5-10 cm from a mature stem and replant directly into nutrient-rich substrate; Tropica notes that 'cut-off shoots will easily start growing when planted.' Cutting anywhere along the stalk reliably stimulates two new lateral shoots to develop at the cutting site, making topping the standard method for thickening a group. Floating cuttings root readily before being planted.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Used for species identity, the P. erectus = P. deccanensis taxonomic clarification, native range (Asia, specifically India), height 15-40 cm, leaf width 1-3 cm, family Lamiaceae, medium light demand, CO2 range 6-14 mg/L, moderate growth rate, background placement, medium care difficulty, propagation by stem cuttings, and the 'intense light keeps it compact' note. Primary parameter source.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirms family Lamiaceae, Asian origin (cites Thailand — likely incorrect; conflicts with Tropica/Flowgrow which both cite southwestern India and which are followed here), medium-to-high lighting, CO2 recommended, pH 6.0-7.5, moderate growth, background placement, medium care level, distinctive 'pointy thin leaves growing from the center stem,' and propagation by cutting new growth with roots or trimming stems anywhere (producing two shoots at the cut site).

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Confirms family Lamiaceae, native range as southwestern India, synonyms Dysophylla erecta Dalzell and Eusteralis erecta (Dalzell) Panigrahi, and most importantly documents the taxonomic clarification: the plant sold in the hobby as 'Pogostemon erectus' is actually P. deccanensis, while the true Dalzell P. erectus is a marginal/terrestrial plant unsuitable for submerged aquarium culture. The hobby retains the 'erectus' trade name.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Pogostemon)
additional.notes: Confirms genus Pogostemon in family Lamiaceae, native to warmer parts of Asia, Africa, and Australia, and that several Pogostemon species (including P. erectus and P. deccanensis) are grown ornamentally in the aquascaping hobby.
